title: Two-Armed Bandit
description: A classic decision-making task from cognitive science.
instructions:
You'll see two buttons (slot machine arms). Each one has a hidden probability of giving you a reward. Your goal is to earn as many points as possible over 80 trials.

On each trial, choose one arm by clicking it or pressing 1/2 (or arrow keys). After each choice, you'll see whether you won a point.

The key challenge: one arm pays out more often than the other, but you need to figure out which one through trial and error. How quickly can you learn?
debrief:
This task measures your exploration-exploitation trade-off — a fundamental concept in reinforcement learning. The Rescorla-Wagner model estimates two parameters from your choices:

• Learning rate (alpha): How quickly you update your beliefs after each outcome. Higher = more responsive to recent outcomes.
• Inverse temperature (tau): How consistently you choose the higher-valued option. Higher = more exploitative, lower = more exploratory.

These same models are used in computational psychiatry to study decision-making differences across clinical populations.
